devdoc.details: Clean-room re-implementation of ENS's `ENSRegistry` (D-06). The bodies and      storage layout below are RNS-authored, but every observable behavior —      the record model, operator approvals, subnode-owner authorization, the      keccak256 subnode derivation, and the `owner()` self-reference clause —      is byte-for-byte ENS-compatible and frozen by D-07.

params.approved: True when the operator is approved, false when revoked.
params.operator: The address being granted or revoked operator rights.
params.owner: The address whose records the operator may manage.

params.label: The labelhash of the subnode being assigned.
params.owner: The address of the subnode's new owner.

params.resolver: The address of the node's new resolver.

params.ttl: The node's new TTL, in seconds.

params.owner: The address of the node's new owner.

owner(bytes32).details: When the registry itself is the stored owner, `owner()` reads back      `address(0)` — the ENS self-reference clause, preserved per D-07.

setResolver(bytes32,address).details: Standalone setter — emits `NewResolver` unconditionally before      storing, matching the frozen ENS behavior.

setSubnodeOwner(bytes32,bytes32,address).details: `public` so `setSubnodeRecord` can call it internally. The subnode      is derived as `keccak256(abi.encodePacked(node, label))` — the exact      namehash derivation, frozen by D-07.

returns._0: The namehash of the affected subnode.

setTTL(bytes32,uint64).details: Standalone setter — emits `NewTTL` unconditionally before storing,      matching the frozen ENS behavior.

operators.details: owner -> operator -> whether the operator may manage all of the      owner's nodes.

devdoc.title: RNSRegistry — the RiseChain Name Service registry.

ApprovalForAll(address,address,bool).notice: Emitted when an operator is granted or revoked blanket rights         over all of an owner's nodes.

NewOwner(bytes32,bytes32,address).notice: Emitted when the owner of a node assigns a new owner to one of         its subnodes.

NewResolver(bytes32,address).notice: Emitted when the resolver for a node changes.

NewTTL(bytes32,uint64).notice: Emitted when the TTL of a node changes.

Transfer(bytes32,address).notice: Emitted when the owner of a node transfers ownership to a new         account.

constructor.notice: Constructs the registry, seating the deployer as the owner of the         root node `0x0`.

isApprovedForAll(address,address).notice: Returns whether `operator` may manage all of `owner`'s nodes.

owner(bytes32).notice: Returns the address that owns the specified node.

recordExists(bytes32).notice: Returns whether a record exists for the specified node.

resolver(bytes32).notice: Returns the resolver address for the specified node.

setApprovalForAll(address,bool).notice: Grants or revokes blanket operator rights over the caller's nodes.

setOwner(bytes32,address).notice: Transfers ownership of a node to a new address.

setRecord(bytes32,address,address,uint64.notice: Sets the owner, resolver, and TTL for an existing node.

setResolver(bytes32,address).notice: Sets the resolver address for a node.

setSubnodeOwner(bytes32,bytes32,address).notice: Creates or transfers a subnode and assigns its owner.

setSubnodeRecord(bytes32,bytes32,address.notice: Creates or updates a subnode and sets its owner, resolver, and TTL.

setTTL(bytes32,uint64).notice: Sets the TTL, in seconds, for a node.

ttl(bytes32).notice: Returns the TTL, in seconds, of the specified node.

userdoc.notice: The authorization spine of RNS: a flat `node -> {owner, resolver, ttl}`         map that every downstream contract reads and writes through the `RNS`         interface. Holds no business logic — pure ownership and authorization.
